slices.focus: Discovering a casual native plant photo habit as stress relief
slices.summary: During a stressful stretch, ducked into a county lunch talk on native plants and started taking phone photos of bioswales and grasses around project sites. The conversation explores how this low-cost, private noticing habit provides a clean mental break from work pressure without becoming another obligation or cataloging system. Keeping it casual with phone photos only and occasional notes.
open_loops: Whether printing a photo would turn the habit into something too precious and pressured
Risk of over-organizing the collection into a formal cataloging system that becomes another task
decisions: Keep the habit casual with phone photos only, no gear commitment or cataloging system
Create a simple phone album to keep photos retrievable without over-organizing
Add occasional notes only for standout images; print only if one lingers in mind for weeks
